items.headline: US Markets Rise on Geopolitical Optimism, Indian Equities End Mixed
items.summary: U.S. stock futures advanced this morning, building on yesterday's record closes for major indices, driven by optimism surrounding a potential U.S.-Iran peace deal. Meanwhile, Indian markets experienced a volatile session, with headline indices closing marginally lower despite early gains, influenced by profit booking and ongoing geopolitical uncertainties. Global oil prices edged below $100 per barrel amidst hopes for easing tensions.

items.headline: Fluence Energy Surges on Earnings Beat
items.summary: Fluence Energy Inc. jumped 31% in pre-market trading after reporting a narrower-than-estimated second-quarter adjusted Ebitda loss and maintaining its 2026 total revenue forecast, which surpassed average analyst estimates.

items.headline: Fortinet Gains on Strong Outlook
items.summary: Cybersecurity firm Fortinet Inc. gained 15% in pre-market after forecasting earnings that beat the average analyst estimate and boosting its full-year revenue outlook, with particular strength noted in its hardware business.

items.headline: DoorDash Jumps on Robust Bookings Forecast
items.summary: DoorDash Inc. jumped 10% in pre-market, driven by a strong forecast for order value in the current period, signaling resilient consumer demand across its mobility and delivery segments in the US and international markets.

items.headline: Fastly Slumps Post-Earnings
items.summary: Software company Fastly Inc. slumped 25% in pre-market trading after its first-quarter earnings report was not strong enough to extend a rally that had lifted shares nearly 300% off a February low.

items.headline: Core Scientific Declines on EPS Miss
items.summary: Core Scientific Inc. declined 6.25% in pre-market after missing first-quarter EPS estimates.

items.headline: US Labor Productivity Rises, Unit Labor Costs Up in Q1 2026
items.summary: Nonfarm business sector labor productivity increased by 0.8% in the first quarter of 2026, while unit labor costs in the nonfarm business sector rose by 2.3% during the same period. This reflects a 3.1% increase in hourly compensation offset by the rise in productivity.

items.headline: Fed Expected to Hold Rates in June Amid Jobless Claims Rise
items.summary: The CME Group's FedWatch tool indicates a 94.1% likelihood that the Federal Reserve will keep current interest rates unchanged during its June meeting. This comes as U.S. jobless claim applications rose by 10,000 to 200,000 last week, although remaining at historically low levels.

items.headline: Viatris, Evergy, Celsius Holdings Report Q1 2026 Results
items.summary: Viatris Inc. ($VTRS) reported total revenues of $3.5 billion, an 8% increase from Q1 2025, and reaffirmed its 2026 financial guidance. Evergy, Inc. ($EVRG) announced Q1 2026 GAAP earnings of $0.64 per share, up from $0.54 in the prior year, and reaffirmed its adjusted EPS guidance for 2026. Celsius Holdings Inc. ($CELH) reported first-quarter 2026 revenue of $423.4 million, a 28% year-over-year increase, with adjusted diluted EPS of $0.23.

items.headline: Utilities, Communication Services Lead Monthly Sector Gains; Consumer Names Lag
items.summary: For May 2026, the Utilities sector has shown strong performance with an average gain of 66%, followed by Communication Services (+40% avg) and Industrials (+35% avg). Conversely, consumer discretionary, staples, healthcare, and housing-adjacent sectors are facing pressure due to the ongoing oil shock, consumer stress, and persistent inflation, while technology, semiconductors, financials, and industrials maintain leadership.

items.headline: US-Iran Peace Deal Hopes Drive Down Oil Prices
items.summary: Optimism regarding a potential U.S.-Iran peace deal has eased geopolitical tensions and contributed to a decline in crude oil prices, with Brent crude edging below $100 per barrel. This development is influencing overall market sentiment.

items.headline: Magnificent 7 Earnings Show Divergence on AI Spending
items.summary: Five of the Magnificent 7 companies reported Q1 2026 earnings this week, all beating analyst estimates. Alphabet ($GOOGL) surged 12% following a strong quarter without significant capital expenditure concerns. However, Meta ($META) saw a 9% drop post-earnings due to its $145 billion AI capital expenditure guidance, and Microsoft ($MSFT) remains down 14.47% year-to-date despite beating estimates, also facing investor concerns over AI spending.
